ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each set of performance metrics */,
  "timestamp_ms" TEXT /* Timestamp in milliseconds when the metrics were recorded */,
  "gnice" REAL /* Amount of CPU time spent in gnice mode */,
  "guest" REAL /* Amount of CPU time spent in guest mode */,
  "idle" REAL /* Amount of CPU time spent idle */,
  "iowait" REAL /* Amount of CPU time spent waiting for I/O operations */,
  "irq" REAL /* Amount of time the CPU spent handling hardware interrupts */,
  "nice" REAL /* Amount of CPU time spent in nice mode */,
  "soft" REAL /* Amount of CPU time spent in softirq */,
  "steal" REAL /* Amount of CPU time spent on stolen time */,
  "sys" REAL /* Amount of CPU time spent in system mode */,
  "usr" REAL /* Amount of CPU time spent in user mode */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server related to these metrics */,
  "memory_total" REAL /* Total memory available on the server */,
  "memory_used" REAL /* Memory currently in use */,
  "memory_free" REAL /* Memory not in use */,
  "disk_read_bytes" TEXT /* Number of bytes read from the disk */,
  "disk_write_bytes" TEXT /* Number of bytes written to the disk */,
  "network_in_bytes" TEXT /* Number of bytes received over the network */,
  "network_out_bytes" TEXT /* Number of bytes sent over the network */,
  "load_1m" REAL /* 1-minute load average */,
  "load_5m" REAL /* 5-minute load average */,
  "load_15m" REAL /* 15-minute load average */,
  "cpu_temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the CPU */,
  "system_uptime" TEXT /* Uptime of the server in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical or logical location of the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the server (e.g., active, inactive, under maintenance) */,
  "hardware_model" TEXT /* Model of the server hardware */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Version of the operating system */,
  "total_cpu_cores" INTEGER /* Total number of CPU cores */,
  "total_memory_gb" REAL /* Total memory in gigabytes */,
  "total_disk_gb" REAL /* Total disk space in gigabytes */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance performed */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "maintenance_status" TEXT /* Current maintenance status (e.g., due, completed, scheduled) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the metrics */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server whose metrics are being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access occurred */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify, delete) */,
  "user_role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user, guest) */,
  "access_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the access event */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  "access_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the access (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user, guest) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server related to the alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., CPU usage, memory usage, disk I/O) */,
  "alert_threshold" REAL /* Threshold value that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved, ignored) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server related to the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "report_generated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the report was generated */,
  "report_generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* JSON data containing the report details */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., generated, pending, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_report_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("report_generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)
